_comment: Full OOS example with entities, workflows, escalations, and claims. This is the JSON representation of a complete Organizational Operating System.

agents.name: Chief of Staff
agents.role: Daily operations coordinator and cross-agent orchestrator
agents.mission: Ensure all agents are aligned and the founder's limited time is optimized

agents.name: Performance Analyst
agents.role: Customer advertising and call center data analyst
agents.mission: Read the numbers across all ad platforms, find patterns, flag what matters

agents.name: Executive Assistant
agents.role: Email triage, draft responses, urgent client escalation
agents.mission: Protect the founder's inbox and ensure no client email goes unanswered

agents.name: Sales Operator
agents.role: Pipeline management, deal tracking, revenue signals
agents.mission: Maintain pipeline health and flag revenue opportunities and risks

agents.name: Retention Guardian
agents.role: Client retention intelligence and churn risk detection
agents.mission: Protect existing client revenue by detecting churn risk early

agents.name: Project Tracker
agents.role: Project status monitoring and delivery risk flagging
agents.mission: Ensure no project deadline is missed and no delivery risk goes undetected

agents.name: Data Architect
agents.role: Data infrastructure, measurement systems, blind spot detection
agents.mission: Ensure clean data flows between all agents and no measurement gap exists

agents.name: Intelligence Scout
agents.role: Frontier scanning for advancements in AI, sales systems, and tooling
agents.mission: Surface net improvements to the agent army's capabilities

steps.action: Pull ad performance data from all platforms, write to shared state
steps.failure_action: Log error, continue briefing without ad data, flag stale data

steps.action: Scan CRM pipeline, write pipeline status to shared state
steps.failure_action: Log error, continue briefing without pipeline data

steps.action: Scan project management platform, write project status to shared state
steps.failure_action: Log error, flag stale project data

steps.action: Scan email inbox, triage client emails, write summary to shared state
steps.failure_action: Log error, flag that inbox was not scanned

steps.action: Read all shared state files, compile into unified daily briefing
steps.output: Daily briefing document with executive summary, urgent items, schedule, systems status
steps.failure_action: Publish partial briefing with warnings about missing data

workflows.name: Client Churn Risk Response
workflows.trigger: Event: Client health score drops below threshold

steps.action: Detect health score decline, classify risk level, generate alert
steps.output: Churn risk alert with client context and recommended actions

steps.action: Check if AGT-004 has pending expansion plays for this client. If yes, pause them.
Draft retention-focused outreach email for human review
steps.output: Draft email in inbox queue awaiting HMN-001 approval
steps.failure_action: Alert HMN-001 that draft could not be generated

steps.action: Review alert, approve/edit email, decide on retention strategy
steps.output: Approved email sent, retention strategy logged
steps.failure_action: If no response in 24 hours, AGT-001 re-escalates

levels.trigger: Any agent encounters an issue outside its authority or cannot resolve an error
levels.authority: Can reprioritize agent work, defer non-urgent items, coordinate between agents

levels.trigger: AGT-001 cannot resolve (requires project timeline change, resource reallocation, or operational decision)
levels.authority: Can change project timelines, reallocate team resources, approve operational changes

levels.trigger: HMN-002 cannot resolve, or issue involves clients, finances, strategy, or external communications
levels.authority: Unlimited. Can override any agent, any decision, any process. Final authority.

conflict_protocols.detection: AGT-004 proposes expansion/upsell to a client that AGT-005 has flagged as at-risk
conflict_protocols.resolution: AGT-005 wins. Retention overrides expansion when client health is declining.
conflict_protocols.escalation: If AGT-004 disputes the health score, escalate to HMN-001 with both perspectives

conflict_protocols.detection: Both agents produce analysis of the same data source with different conclusions
conflict_protocols.resolution: AGT-007 owns data architecture (what data means structurally). AGT-002 owns performance analysis (what data means operationally). If the scope overlap is unclear, AGT-001 arbitrates.
conflict_protocols.escalation: AGT-001 arbitrates. If unresolved, escalate to HMN-001.

override_rules.condition: Client health score drops below defined threshold
override_rules.scope: All expansion, upsell, and cross-sell actions for the at-risk client
All agent actions and decisions without exception

decisions.domain: Ad spend changes and budget reallocation

dependencies.description: Chief of Staff reads all shared state files to compile briefings
Performance Analyst pulls data from ad platform APIs
Churn risk workflow is triggered by Retention Guardian's health score monitoring
All emails drafted by Executive Assistant require CEO approval before sending
Both access CRM data for the same clients. Override rule OVR-001 governs conflicts.

claims.rule: Every agent writes to a shared state file. No agent reads data sources directly.
claims.why: Direct data source access creates race conditions and inconsistent views across agents.
claims.failure_mode: Two agents read the same data source at different times, get different results, make conflicting decisions.
claims.evidence_reference: Incident 2026-01-15: reporting agent and campaign agent queried Meta API simultaneously, produced conflicting spend numbers. Resolved by implementing shared state pattern.
claims.scope: All agents. Does not apply to initial data collection job.

claims.rule: Only one agent has authority to send external communications.
claims.why: Multiple agents sending emails creates duplicate communications and confused clients.
claims.failure_mode: Two agents both draft and send a client update. Client receives contradictory information.
claims.evidence_reference: Observed 3 times in first month of operation before this rule was established.
claims.scope: All external-facing communications. Internal agent messaging is unrestricted.

claims.rule: The analytics agent reports patterns but never recommends actions.
claims.why: When analytics recommended actions, recommendations were ignored because they lacked client context.
claims.failure_mode: Analytics agent recommends budget increase. Account manager knows client has cash flow issues. Client loses trust.
claims.evidence_reference: Three incidents where analytics recommendations were overridden by account managers with client context.
claims.scope: Analytics and reporting functions. Strategy agents may recommend within their authority.

claims.rule: The retention agent overrides the sales agent when a client is flagged at risk.
claims.why: Sales expansion to at-risk client accelerates churn. Retention must take priority.
claims.failure_mode: Sales agent proposes upsell to declining client. Client cancels.
claims.evidence_reference: One near-miss where expansion play was paused 2 hours before client expressed frustration.
claims.scope: Applies when client health score drops below threshold. Does not apply to healthy accounts.

claims.rule: All agent output is logged to an audit trail before any action is taken.
claims.why: Without audit trails, diagnosing agent errors requires guessing.
claims.failure_mode: Agent makes error. Without logs, team spends hours debugging. With logs, root cause found in minutes.
claims.evidence_reference: Measured: debugging time decreased from avg 3 hours to avg 12 minutes after audit trail implementation.
claims.scope: All agents, all actions. No exceptions.

claims.rule: Each agent has a written one-line role statement and a list of authorized actions.
claims.why: Without explicit authority boundaries, agents drift into overlapping responsibilities.
claims.failure_mode: Two agents update the same project status. Conflicting updates confuse the team.
claims.evidence_reference: Overlap between project tracker and analytics agent resolved by explicit role definitions.
claims.scope: All agents. Authority documents reviewed monthly.

claims.rule: No agent modifies another agent's shared state file.
claims.why: Shared state files are single-writer. Multiple writers cause data races.
claims.failure_mode: Project agent and billing agent both write to client status file. One overwrites the other.
claims.evidence_reference: Data corruption incident resolved by enforcing single-writer pattern. Zero corruption since.
claims.scope: All shared state files. Agents may read any state but only write to their own.

claims.rule: Agents coordinate through shared state files, not direct messaging.
claims.why: Direct messaging creates hidden dependencies. Shared state makes coordination visible.
claims.failure_mode: Agent A messages Agent B directly. Message not logged. When B fails, team cannot trace the cause.
claims.evidence_reference: Inferred from visibility benefits of shared state pattern (C001, C007). Not yet stress-tested at scale.
claims.scope: Production coordination. Development may use direct messaging.

claims.rule: Cross-agent workflows use a publish-subscribe pattern through shared state.
claims.why: Pub/sub decouples agents. Any agent can consume shared state without the publisher knowing.
claims.failure_mode: Tightly coupled agents create cascading failures. One goes down, all dependents fail.
claims.evidence_reference: Daily briefing workflow (WFL-001) survives individual scanner failures because of pub/sub pattern.
claims.scope: All multi-agent workflows. Single-agent tasks do not require pub/sub.

claims.rule: If an agent encounters an unresolvable error, it logs the error and stops. No automatic retry.
claims.why: Automatic retries on unresolvable errors create noise and mask root cause.
claims.failure_mode: Agent retries failed API call 100 times, consuming rate limits and generating 100 error logs.
claims.evidence_reference: One incident where ad platform rate limit was consumed by retry loop.
claims.scope: All agents. Temporary network errors may be retried once with backoff.

claims.rule: Giving the analytics agent write access to campaign settings produced strategically wrong optimizations.
claims.why: Analytics agent optimized for metrics the client did not prioritize.
claims.failure_mode: Agent decreases spend on a brand-building campaign. Client is frustrated by the uninstructed change.
claims.evidence_reference: Incident where analytics agent reduced spend on client's strategic brand campaign. Reverted. Write access removed.
claims.scope: Any agent with optimization authority. Require human approval for strategic changes.

claims.rule: A single shared state file for all agents became a bottleneck and source of merge conflicts.
claims.why: Single file means every update blocks every other agent.
claims.failure_mode: Two agents update simultaneously. One update lost. State becomes inconsistent.
claims.evidence_reference: Measured: 12 state corruption events in first month with single file. Zero after switching to per-agent files.
claims.scope: All shared state. Per-agent files resolved the issue.

claims.rule: All external communications require human approval before sending.
claims.why: AI communications can be tonally wrong, factually incorrect, or strategically misaligned.
claims.failure_mode: Agent sends email with incorrect performance numbers. Client loses trust.
claims.evidence_reference: Founding rule. Non-negotiable. Established before first agent was deployed.
claims.scope: All external communications. Internal logging and status updates are autonomous.

claims.rule: Pricing, contracts, and financial commitments require human decision-making.
claims.why: Financial decisions have legal and relationship implications AI agents cannot fully assess.
claims.failure_mode: Agent applies discount violating margin floors. Retained client becomes unprofitable.
claims.evidence_reference: No incident has occurred. Rule is precautionary based on known risk patterns in automated pricing.
claims.scope: All financial decisions. Agents may calculate and recommend but never execute.
